text: Which of the following describes the behavior of a 'Standard' scaling policy in a multi-cluster warehouse?

options.a: It minimizes the number of clusters to save credits.
options.b: It starts a new cluster immediately when a query is queued.
options.c: It waits 2 minutes before starting a second cluster.
options.d: It only scales up the size of the existing cluster.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows you to query data from a 'Share' even if your virtual warehouse is suspended (without resuming it).
What is the primary purpose of the 'SYSTEM$ALLOWLIST' function?

options.a: To see which users are allowed to log in.
options.b: To retrieve the hostnames and port numbers needed to configure a firewall for Snowflake access.
options.c: To manage the IP addresses in a Network Policy.
options.d: To list the cloud provider regions Snowflake is available in.

text: Which of the following is true regarding 'Internal Stages'?

options.a: They are managed by the customer in their own S3 bucket.
options.b: They are automatically provided by Snowflake and stored within the Snowflake account.
options.c: They do not incur any storage costs.
options.d: They can only be accessed using the Snowflake Web UI.

text: What is the maximum number of days for 'Time Travel' retention for a Permanent table in Enterprise edition?
True or False: Snowflake handles the partitioning of data automatically; users do not define partition keys.
Which Snowflake component provides the results for a query if the results are already in the cache and the data hasn't changed?
What is the impact of reducing a Warehouse size while it is running queries?

options.b: The warehouse size remains the same until current queries finish, then it scales down.
options.d: The warehouse is suspended and restarted.

text: Which role is required to execute the 'UNDROP' command on a database?

options.c: A role with OWNERSHIP privilege on the database.

text: True or False: All data stored in Snowflake is encrypted at rest by default.
What is the purpose of a 'Stream' object?

options.a: To load data continuously from a stage.
options.b: To track Change Data Capture (CDC) information for a table.
options.c: To connect Snowflake to a streaming service like Kafka.

text: Which command is used to load data into Snowflake from a file on a local machine?
True or False: A single Virtual Warehouse can be shared across multiple Snowflake accounts.
Which Snowflake edition includes support for PrivateLink and HIPAA compliance?
What does the 'ON_ERROR = CONTINUE' option do in a COPY INTO command?

options.a: It stops the load at the first error.
options.b: It ignores any errors and loads the rest of the valid data from the files.
options.c: It retries the file that had an error.
options.d: It logs the error and stops the warehouse.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Materialized Views' to improve the performance of queries that use the same subquery frequently.
Which function is used to check if a specific JSON key exists in a VARIANT column?
What is the primary benefit of 'Clustering' a table?

options.b: It improves query performance by enabling more effective pruning.
options.d: It allows multiple users to write to the same row.

text: Which view is used to monitor the credit consumption of Snowpipe?
True or False: A Snowflake account can have only one 'Resource Monitor'.
Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization Service'?

options.a: It is a free service in all editions.
options.b: It creates a persistent data structure to speed up point lookups.
options.c: It is used to search for files in a stage.
options.d: It optimizes the Snowflake Web UI search bar.

text: What is the default retention period for 'Fail-safe' in Snowflake?
Which command allows you to view the definition of a view?
True or False: You can apply a 'Masking Policy' to a column in a table.
What is the maximum number of clusters in a 'Multi-cluster Warehouse'?

options.d: 10 by default (can be increased by support)

text: Which layer of the Snowflake architecture handles 'Metadata' storage?
True or False: A 'Transient' table survives a system failure and is persisted across sessions.
Which command is used to unload data from a Snowflake table to an external stage?
What is the primary benefit of 'External Tables'?

options.a: They are faster than internal tables.
options.b: They allow you to query data in cloud storage without first loading it into Snowflake.
options.c: They do not require a Virtual Warehouse.
options.d: They have 90 days of Time Travel automatically.

text: Which view provides history of all queries executed in an account for the last 365 days?
True or False: Snowflake credits are consumed per second (with a 60-second minimum).
Which tool would you use to find out why a query is running slowly?
What is the purpose of 'Object Tagging'?

options.b: To group and track objects for cost management and compliance.
options.d: To share data with other accounts.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Directory Tables' for cataloging unstructured data.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Shares' in your account?
What is the maximum Time Travel retention for 'Transient' tables?
Which role is the only one that can manage 'ORGADMIN'?
True or False: Data shared via a 'Share' can be updated by the consumer.
What does 'Auto-resume' do for a Warehouse?

options.a: It restarts the warehouse if it crashes.
options.b: It automatically starts the warehouse when a query is submitted to it.

text: Which Snowflake edition is required for 'Database Replication' across accounts?
True or False: Snowflake allows the creation of 'Secure User-Defined Functions (UDFs)'.
Which command is used to add a clustering key to a table?
What is the purpose of 'Access History'?

options.a: To see who logged into the account.
options.b: To track which users have accessed specific data in tables/views (Governance).
options.d: To see the files uploaded to a stage.

text: True or False: 'Zero-copy cloning' can be used on a Schema.
Which Snowflake interface is a web-based UI for data analysis and visualization?
What is the default character used to separate fields in a CSV file in Snowflake?
True or False: Snowflake 'Snowpark' can be used to build data pipelines using Python.
Which view allows you to see the list of all 'Tasks' in an account?
What does 'Pruning' refer to in Snowflake?

options.b: Skipping micro-partitions that do not contain relevant data for a query.

text: True or False: 'MFA' is enabled by default for all Snowflake users.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Pipes' in your account?
What is the primary benefit of 'Snowpipe'?

options.a: It is faster than the COPY command.
options.b: It provides near real-time data ingestion from cloud storage.
options.c: It does not require a Warehouse.

text: True or False: A 'Task' can be part of a 'Directed Acyclic Graph' (DAG) of tasks.
Which view provides history of all data loads performed using the COPY command in the last 64 days?
What happens if a Virtual Warehouse is 'Suspended' and a new query is submitted to it?

options.b: The warehouse automatically resumes (if auto-resume is ON) and then runs the query.
options.c: The query is queued until a user manually starts the warehouse.
options.d: The query is moved to a different warehouse.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Policy Tags' to manage data masking policies.
Which command allows you to view the privileges granted to a specific user?
What is the impact of 'Auto-clustering' on table availability?

options.a: The table is locked while clustering.
options.b: The table remains available for querying and DML during the process.
options.c: The table is read-only during clustering.

text: True or False: Snowflake 'Stored Procedures' can be written in Javascript.
Which Snowflake edition is required for 'Multi-cluster Warehouse' support?
What is the primary function of 'Resource Monitors'?

options.b: To set limits on credit consumption and notify admins or suspend warehouses.

text: True or False: Snowflake supports 'External Functions' which allow calling an API outside Snowflake.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Stages'?
What is the maximum file size recommended for data loading in Snowflake?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Row Access Policies' to filter rows based on user role.
Which view provides history of all 'Task' executions in an account?
What is the default T-shirt size for a new Virtual Warehouse if not specified?
True or False: A 'Secure View' protects the underlying SQL query from being seen by unauthorized users.
Which command is used to see the 'Clustering Depth' of a table?
What is the purpose of 'Dynamic Data Masking'?

options.b: To obscure sensitive data in query results based on the user's role/permissions.

text: True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the individual Warehouse level.
Which Snowflake interface is a command-line tool?
What does 'DML' stand for in Snowflake?
True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Tri-Secret Secure' for customer-managed encryption keys.
Which command is used to see the 'DDL' of an existing Snowflake table?
